title: BTC->SOL/ETH intraday lead-lag check (cheap)
source: quant-phd-advisor literature survey 2026-05-29 (intraday edges on FX/metals/crypto at H1). See project-no-edge-h4-primary-direct memory + hackathon plan.
why: Information enters via BTC, propagates to less-liquid alts with lag (Granger BTC->alts; RIBAF 2019, APFM 2026). SOL most illiquid -> largest lag.
value_rationale: Edge-search for the hackathon competition book (H1, conservative vol-target). B0080 is the front-runner (best OOS evidence across all asset classes) and is spiked first.
scope: Cheap 30-min check: cross-correlate BTC H1 returns with SOL/ETH at lags 0,+-1,+-2; confirm corr(BTC_t, SOL_t+1) > corr(SOL_t, BTC_t+1) (true lead) AND 'long SOL when BTC up last hour' hit-rate>53%.
Assets: SOL (best), ETH (marginal). Caveat: lead mostly lives sub-hourly; likely decayed to ~0 at H1 -- if M15/M5 available, test there. Spike last, do not over-invest. Expected gross 0.3-0.8.

history.reason: registered by hackathon-pm from quant-phd-advisor shortlist
feat(backlog): register intraday-edge shortlist B0080-B0083 from quant-phd-advisor survey
BTC->alt lead-lag: no true lead (corr_fwd~0, not asymmetric), sub-coinflip hit-rate, net negative H1+M15 (spike b0083)
feat(spike): crypto/lead-lag/pairs edges all fail OOS; edge search exhausted
